news.title: STAT+: Eli Lilly says Verve’s gene editor lowers cholesterol levels in early study
news.summary: Eli Lilly said Monday that a high dose of its gene-editing therapy reduced cholesterol levels by 62% in participants in a clinical trial.

news.title: How specialty practices can get more out of technology investments
news.summary: NextGen Healthcare on why specialty practices that connect their technology are seeing real gains in efficiency, patient experience and revenue.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Abridge taps former Slack exec as new CTO; Alignment Healthcare shuffles leadership team
news.summary: Welcome to this week's Chutes & Ladders, our roundup of hirings, firings and retirings throughout the industry. Please submit the good news—or the bad—from your shop, and we will feature it here at the end of each week.

news.title: ACAP warns final ACA rule adds further uncertainty to a market in flux
news.summary: The Trump administration recently finalized a major regulation governing the Affordable Care Act's marketplaces, and community health plans are expressing concern about the impact the changes could have on consumers.

news.title: Quorum Health transitioning to nonprofit for financial pickup
news.summary: The 11-hospital system made it clear that its transaction, set to close in the fall, is in pursuit of new streams of financial support.

news.title: Innovaccer picks up CaduceusHealth to offer end-to-end revenue cycle management
news.summary: Innovaccer acquired CaduceusHealth to combine its AI platform with the company's revenue cycle management services and staff to serve ambulatory care providers.

news.title: Women's Health Capitol Hill Day: Advocates lobby to advance budget priorities
news.summary: A Thursday gathering on Capitol Hill was organized by Women’s Health Advocates (WHA), a rapidly growing bipartisan group founded by lobbyist Liz Powell.

news.title: Ksana Health awarded $17.9M to build behavioral health foundation model
news.summary: The software company is partnering with several health systems, including Providence, on the initiative to train AI models from smartphone and wearable data for mental health.

news.title: Industry Voices—From claims to compassion: Reclaiming patient advocacy in revenue cycle
news.summary: Working with payers to ensure every patient is covered at the appropriate level of care isn't a back-office function—it's the place where revenue cycle managers can show up for patients who aren't in the room.

news.title: Health Tech Weekly Rundown: Prime Healthcare expands virtual sitting tech; CVS Health studies seniors' digital health needs
news.summary: Stay up to date on the latest in health tech, digital health and health AI news with this weekly brief.

news.title: Hospitals allege contracted CVS Health subsidiaries pocketed their 340B savings
news.summary: Lawsuits filed by nonprofit hospitals accuse the company's pharmacy benefit manager, specialty pharmacy and third-party administrator of working together to artificially depress rates for 340B claims and pocket the spread.

news.title: Freestanding EDs, urgent care acquisition opportunities abound for HCA Healthcare
news.summary: The for-profit spent about $260 million on outpatient purchases during this year's first quarter, and expects a favorable acquisition environment for these sites to continue despite potential policy headwinds, leadership said Wednesday.

news.title: Study: Brokers increasingly recommending ICHRA to employers
news.summary: ICHRA has been gaining steam, and a new survey suggests that these plans may be hitting the mainstream.

news.title: Lilly preps for ph. 2 launch after Verve gene editor cuts cholesterol on par with PCSK9 inhibitors
news.summary: Eli Lilly is laying the groundwork for a phase 2 trial of the lipid-lowering gene editor it picked up from Verve Therapeutics, with the candidate now showing strong and consistent cholesterol reductions after a higher dosing regimen.

news.title: BioAge CEO talks NLRP3 and ‘pipeline in a pill’ ambitions
news.summary: Recorded at Fierce Biotech Week in Boston, this episode of “The Top Line” features a conversation with BioAge co-founder and CEO Kristen Fortney on aging biology, inflammation and why BioAge believes its oral NLRP3 inhibitor could have applications across multiple diseases.

news.title: Chutes & Ladders—Former FDA leader takes new biotech Turn
news.summary: Stephen Hahn, M.D., who served as the commissioner of the FDA for just over a year during the first Trump administration, is taking his extensive regulatory expertise to Turn Therapeutics. As executive clinical and regulatory lead, Hahn will guide the regulatory strategy for GX-03, Turn’s topical eczema treatment that is currently in a phase 2 study.

news.title: Has Novartis’ T-Charge been overtaken by in vivo CAR-Ts? Execs argue there’s room for both
news.summary: It’s been more than four years since Novartis shook up the CAR-T space with its T-Charge platform. But that’s a lifetime in the fast-moving world of cell therapies.

news.title: OSE turns up the heat on ovarian cancer with phase 2, Keytruda-supported win
news.summary: OSE Immunotherapeutics has shared phase 2 data on its neo-epitope vaccine candidate and Keytruda in ovarian cancer, linking maintenance use of the combination to improvements over best supportive care.

news.title: Biogen axes Denali-partnered Parkinson’s prospect after phase 2 flop
news.summary: A phase 2b trial of Biogen and Denali Therapeutics’ Parkinson’s disease candidate has missed its primary endpoint, prompting the partners to end development in the idiopathic form of the condition.

news.title: ASCO: Merck, Kelun's sac-TMT ADC combo beats Keytruda by 65% on progression in first-line lung cancer
news.summary: According to an abstract released ahead of the ASCO 2026 annual meeting, combining the TROP2-directed ADC with Keytruda slashed the risk of disease progression or death by a whopping 65% compared with Keytruda alone in treatment-naïve, PD-L1-positive non-small cell lung cancer.

news.title: Betting on biotech: Prediction markets set sights on clinical trials
news.summary: Several biotech contracts are rotating through Kalshi and Polymarket, while a prediction market specifically focused on clinical trials has recently launched.

news.title: Lilly’s triple-G drug helps patients lose roughly a quarter of weight, showcasing competitive profile
news.summary: As Eli Lilly looks to hold its lead in the lucrative obesity market, the company’s next-gen prospect, the triple-G drug retatrutide, has met the primary endpoint for weight loss in another pivotal study. Though the results show competitive efficacy and tolerability, they may leave some observers slightly underwhelmed based on high expectations.

news.title: Struggling Liminatus pays $320M in stock for CAR-T biotech InnocsAI
news.summary: Cancer immunotherapy company Liminatus Pharma has agreed to pay out shares worth $320 million to buy CAR-T biotech InnocsAI.

news.title: Infex, following in Insmed’s footsteps, advances lung disease candidate after clearing phase 2a
news.summary: Infex Therapeutics is pushing ahead with development of a non-cystic fibrosis bronchiectasis drug candidate after posting phase 2a data, advancing efforts to add to the options for treating patients who recently gained access to Insmed’s Brinsupri.

news.title: US taps small San Diego biotech for experimental Ebola treatment as epidemic worsens
news.summary: As an outbreak of Ebola in central Africa continues to worsen, the federal government has tapped a little-known biotech in San Diego to provide doses of an experimental antibody that may be used to treat the deadly virus.

news.title: 大模型第一梯队来了新玩家，市场还没反应过来？
news.summary: 小参数大野心，云知声要抢第一梯队？

news.title: 谁搬空了拉勾？
news.summary: 一个「垂直」标签的13年保质期

news.title: 上海，跑出一个手术机器人IPO
news.summary: 拿到赴港上市的“通行证”。

news.title: 实业资本“围猎”五星级酒店
news.summary: 逆周期抄底，打得什么算盘？
